release_notes:
Alpha 5.0.74 - 2026-05-15 - FOUR Pablo issues in one release.

Pablo, all in one message:
"sigue dando exactamente el mismo error de seguridad. sigue
siendo inutil todo porque siempre da el erroe de seguridad
de windows, como se arregla? además, a otros usuarios CON
PYTHON INSTALADO les da este error 'Python is not installed
or not in PATH'. también este usuario trata de abrir la app
... cuando abre la versión adjunta, dice que es la versión
63 pero esta debería ser la 73. Qué coño estás haciendo???
además el updater no funciona, cuando le da a restart, no se
le reinicia con la versión actualizada."

(1) Version drift -- "dice que es la versión 63 pero esta
debería ser la 73"
-----------------------------------------------------------
src/qt_app/__init__.py had ``__qt_milestone__ = "5.0.63"`` as
a HARDCODED string. The Qt UI displays this in the title bar /
About / Publish dialog. Every release since 5.0.63 forgot to
bump it, so users saw a stale version even when running brand-
new code.

FIX: ``__qt_milestone__`` now derives from
``src.__version__`` via a small ``_derive_qt_milestone()``
helper at module load time. The two CANNOT drift again --
there is exactly one source of truth.

(2) "Python is not installed or not in PATH" on machines
that HAVE Python
-----------------------------------------------------------
The .cmd launchers only tried ``py``. Users with python.exe
but no py.exe (Microsoft Store install, custom pip-only
installs, corporate IT skipping the Windows Python Launcher
MSI) hit the "not installed" error.

FIX: every launcher (run_qt.cmd, run.cmd, the standalone
'Install PySide6.cmd') now resolves a PYCMD variable by
trying ``py`` -> ``python`` -> ``python3`` in order. The
first one that responds to --version wins and is used for the
rest of the script (pip install, app invoke, etc.). Any of
the three on PATH is enough.

(3) Windows SmartScreen "Windows protected your PC" -- every
launch
-----------------------------------------------------------
When users download a .zip from the internet and extract,
Windows attaches a Zone.Identifier alternate data stream
(the Mark of the Web) to every file. SmartScreen + Defender
block .cmd execution on every launch.

FIX (two paths):
a. START-HERE.txt now has "STEP 0 -- Unblock the ZIP
BEFORE extracting" as the cleanest fix: right-click the
.zip -> Properties -> tick Unblock -> OK -> extract.
Every extracted file inherits no-MOTW.
b. For users who already extracted, a new top-level
"Unblock files (run if Windows blocks the launcher).cmd"
script runs PowerShell's Unblock-File on every file in
the tree (Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File | Unblock-File).

(4) Updater "Restart Now" lands on the OLD version
-----------------------------------------------------------
Pre-5.0.74 the inline cmd swap blocks in run_qt.cmd and
run.cmd hardcoded ``_update_staging\src``. The new 5.0.69+
ZIP layout puts the staged src under
``_update_staging\app\src``. The condition
``if exist "_update_staging\src"`` returned false, the swap
silently skipped, AND the ``.marosa_update_pending`` flag was
only deleted INSIDE the (skipped) swap block. So the user
saw the OLD version on relaunch + the persistent "update
pending" state.

Plus: marosa_magic.py::_apply_pending_update's file list
only covered the legacy Tk files (marosa_magic.py,
requirements.txt, run.cmd, version.json). It didn't swap
marosa_qt.py / requirements_qt.txt / run_qt.cmd /
recover.cmd / bootstrap_updater.py / box_mappings/, so even
when src/ DID swap correctly the user got a half-applied
update.

FIX:
- marosa_magic.py::_apply_pending_update file list expanded
to all 5.0+ files + box_mappings/ directory swap.
- run_qt.cmd + run.cmd inline cmd swap REPLACED by a
one-line invocation of marosa_magic._apply_pending_update
via ``%PYCMD% -c "..."``. The Python helper handles every
staged-layout variant (flat src, wrapped-by-release-
folder, wrapped-by-app-folder) via _find_staged_root, AND
has the comprehensive file list.

Tests
=====

tests/test_1005_qt_574_version_and_updater.py (NEW, 11 tests):
- __qt_milestone__ matches __version__ (no drift).
- __qt_milestone__ is derived at runtime, not a hardcoded
string.
- run_qt.cmd / run.cmd / install_pyside6.cmd all resolve
PYCMD via py -> python -> python3, and every real invoke
uses %PYCMD% (not hardcoded py).
- START-HERE explains STEP 0 (right-click unblock).
- UNBLOCK_FILES_CMD uses PowerShell Unblock-File on the
whole tree.
- _apply_pending_update finds the new app/ subfolder layout
AND swaps marosa_qt.py + run_qt.cmd + requirements_qt.txt
+ bootstrap_updater.py.
- _apply_pending_update swaps the box_mappings/ directory
too.
- run_qt.cmd + run.cmd delegate the swap to the Python
helper, NOT the broken inline cmd block.

11/11 green.

Net 5.0.74 archive vs 5.0.73:
- 4 top-level files instead of 3 (added the Unblock script).
- Layout: 1-START-HERE.txt + Launch Marosa Magic Tool.cmd
+ Install PySide6 (run if launch crashes).cmd +
Unblock files (run if Windows blocks the launcher).cmd
+ app/.

===============================================================
